title: Compactness Problems in Counting Special Lagrangians and Fueter Sections
speaker: Saman Habibi Esfahani
abstract: This talk is based on joint work with Yang Li. I will discuss the problem of counting special Lagrangians in Calabi-Yau 3-folds and Fueter sections to define new numerical and Floer-theoretic invariants. The key challenges are the non-compactness problems and the wall-crossing phenomenon. Donaldson-Segal proposed a weighted count of special Lagrangians to remedy this problem, where the weight is determined by counting Fueter sections. However, the compactness of the space of Fueter sections is an open problem, first raised by Taubes in 1998, motivated by defining new invariants of 3-manifolds. In this work, we prove a compactness theorem for Fueter sections and also, if time permits, a local version of the Donaldson-Scaduto conjecture. This conjecture is expected to play a major role in proving compactness results for special Lagrangians in Lefschetz-fibered Calabi-Yau 3-folds.

proof_stages.label: Calabi-Yau and special Lagrangian setup
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines Calabi-Yau threefolds and special Lagrangian threefolds, explains their minimality and index-zero deformation problem, and recalls McLean's theorem that nearby compact special Lagrangians have dimension b_1(L).

proof_stages.label: Counting proposals and wall-crossing obstruction
proof_stages.summary: The speaker contrasts Joyce's b_1=0 counting proposal with perturbative transversality approaches, then argues that compactness failure and wall crossing force two main questions: which singularities occur and whether weighted counts can be invariant.

proof_stages.label: Adiabatic K3-fibration reduction to trivalent graphs
proof_stages.summary: Donaldson-Scaduto's approach restricts to K3-fibered Calabi-Yau threefolds and collapses the K3 fibers, under which special Lagrangians are expected to project to trivalent graphs whose combinatorics encodes wall crossing.

proof_stages.label: Cylindrical edges and pair-of-pants vertices
proof_stages.summary: The trivalent-graph picture predicts cylindrical special Lagrangians over edges and pair-of-pants special Lagrangians over vertices, leading to the Donaldson-Scaduto conjecture that three asymptotic cylindrical ends can be glued to a pair of pants.

proof_stages.label: Local Donaldson-Scaduto existence, regularity, and uniqueness
proof_stages.summary: For the local degenerate hyperkähler model, the speaker states joint theorems with Yang Li proving existence and uniqueness of the pair-of-pants special Lagrangian, via a singular real Monge-Ampere equation plus geometric-measure-theoretic regularity.

proof_stages.label: Weighted counts via Fueter sections
proof_stages.summary: To replace naive special-Lagrangian counts, the proposed weight of a compact special Lagrangian is the count of Fueter sections into the Atiyah-Hitchin hyperkähler four-manifold, motivated by Donaldson-Segal's Calabi-Yau monopole conjecture and related Taubes invariants.

proof_stages.label: Bounded-energy compactness for Fueter sections
proof_stages.summary: Assuming uniform Dirichlet-energy and L-infinity bounds, Walpuski-type compactness gives convergence away from possible nonremovable points and codimension-two bubbling, and for the Atiyah-Hitchin target the speaker's theorem rules out these defects.

proof_stages.label: Unbounded-energy degeneration and Z2 harmonic one-forms
proof_stages.summary: Because Fueter-section energy is not topologically bounded and may blow up, rescaling divergent sequences leads to Z_2 harmonic one-forms on the three-manifold, suggesting a compactification rather than an honest compactness theorem.

examples.note: The speaker compares counting special Lagrangians in Calabi-Yau threefolds with Gromov-Witten-type counts of holomorphic curves, making the intended invariant-counting problem familiar without supplying a proof step.
